text.title: shell-productivity Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for shell-productivity as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding eval $(starship init bash) to a non-interactive shell init file like /etc/environment or a service ExecStartPre.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: starship requires an interactive TTY. Running it in non-interactive contexts (systemd services, cron jobs) either fails silently or adds unusable ANSI escape codes to the environment.

failure-mode.description: Overriding cat with bat globally in scripts that parse command output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: bat adds ANSI color codes and paging that break programmatic parsing. Gate the alias with command -v and use \cat or command cat in scripts to bypass it.
